title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
summary: Kobie Crawford argues that tool discipline and behavior training matter more than model scale for query tasks. A 4B Snorkel-finetuned model with RL outperforms Qwen 3 235B by using proper tool invocation (get_table_name, schema inspection, self-correction) rather than reasoning depth, achieving this with under $500 training cost.
writeup: Kobie Crawford of Snorkel argues that for query tasks, tool discipline and behavior training matter more than raw model scale. A 4B Snorkel-finetuned model trained with reinforcement learning outperforms Qwen 3 235B by invoking tools properly (get_table_name, schema inspection, self-correction) instead of relying on reasoning depth. The training cost came in under $500. The takeaway: targeted behavioral training on small models can beat much larger ones on structured query work.
